# Reference

The low-level surface that every [module](/modules/) is built on. Reach
for these primitives when you're writing your own integration, gluing
together modules, or doing one-off file/symlink/exec work.

```lua
local rb = require("rootbeer")
```

## Core

| Page | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| [Core API](/reference/core) | File ops, symlinks, exec, secrets, profile system. |
| [Host](/reference/host) | The `rb.host` table — OS, arch, hostname, user. |

## Data Formats

| Page | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| [`json`](/formats/json) | Pretty-printed with 2-space indent. |
| [`toml`](/formats/toml) | Datetimes decode as strings. |
| [`yaml`](/formats/yaml) | Tags decode transparently to scalars. |
| [`plist`](/formats/plist) | XML output; decode accepts XML or binary. |

## Script Writers

| Page | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| [Script writers](/scripts/) | `rb.scripts.bash` / `python` / `script` … |
